---
title: "Research"
subtitle: "Publications and ongoing research by Murat Abus on political behavior, election science, public administration, political communication, public opinion, local politics, and gender in politics."
---

```{=html}
<div class="pub-list">

<div class="section-heading">Journal Articles</div>

<div class="pub-card">
  <div class="pub-number">5.</div>
  <div class="pub-content">
    <div class="pub-title-line">
      <span class="pub-title">Administrative Unit Proliferation in Parliamentary Systems: Evidence from Turkish Elections, 1960–2018</span>
      <span class="pub-journal-inline">Electoral Studies (2026)</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-authors"><strong>Murat Abus</strong> &amp; Sabri Ciftci</div>
    <div class="pub-links">
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.electstud.2026.103087" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"/><polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"/><line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"/></svg> DOI</a>
      <a href="uploads/Abus-Ciftci_2026 Administrative Unit Proliferation in Parliamentary Systems.pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/><polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/><line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13"/><line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17"/></svg> PDF</a>
      <a href="uploads/Abus-Ciftci_2026 Supplemental Material.pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M21.44 11.05l-9.19 9.19a6 6 0 0 1-8.49-8.49l9.19-9.19a4 4 0 0 1 5.66 5.66l-9.2 9.19a2 2 0 0 1-2.83-2.83l8.49-8.48"/></svg> Supplementary</a>
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/1NDCXM" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><ellipse cx="12" cy="5" rx="9" ry="3"/><path d="M21 12c0 1.66-4 3-9 3s-9-1.34-9-3"/><path d="M3 5v14c0 1.66 4 3 9 3s9-1.34 9-3V5"/></svg> Data &amp; Code</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleAbstract('abs1', this); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="3" y1="6" x2="21" y2="6"/><line x1="3" y1="12" x2="15" y2="12"/><line x1="3" y1="18" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg> Summary</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleCite('cite1'); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M14.017 21v-7.391c0-5.704 3.731-9.57 8.983-10.609l.995 2.151c-2.432.917-3.995 3.638-3.995 5.849h4v10h-9.983zm-14.017 0v-7.391c0-5.704 3.748-9.57 9-10.609l.996 2.151c-2.433.917-3.996 3.638-3.996 5.849h3.983v10h-9.983z"/></svg> Cite</a>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-abstract" id="abs1">
      We argue that in parliamentary systems, electoral motives matter as in presidential systems, but proliferation decisions also depend on government type. Single-party governments are more likely to pursue proliferation to secure electoral success, especially under electoral uncertainty. Using original district-level splits data in Turkey from 1960–2018, we conduct random intercept logistic regression models to test these expectations.
    </div>
    <div class="cite-block" id="cite1">
<pre>@article{abus2026administrative,
  title   = {Administrative Unit Proliferation in Parliamentary Systems:
             Evidence from {Turkish} Elections, 1960--2018},
  author  = {Abus, Murat and Ciftci, Sabri},
  journal = {Electoral Studies},
  year    = {2026},
  pages   = {103087},
  volume  = {101}
}</pre>
      <button onclick="copyBib('cite1')">Copy</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-card">
  <div class="pub-number">4.</div>
  <div class="pub-content">
    <div class="pub-title-line">
      <span class="pub-title">The Gendered Politics of Candidate Selection: An Analysis of Left-Right Preferences in Parliamentary Democracies</span>
      <span class="pub-journal-inline">Political Research Quarterly (2025)</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-authors"><strong>Murat Abus</strong></div>
    <div class="pub-links">
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.1177/10659129251340227" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"/><polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"/><line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"/></svg> DOI</a>
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/VUAFFF" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><ellipse cx="12" cy="5" rx="9" ry="3"/><path d="M21 12c0 1.66-4 3-9 3s-9-1.34-9-3"/><path d="M3 5v14c0 1.66 4 3 9 3s9-1.34 9-3V5"/></svg> Data &amp; Code</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleAbstract('abs2', this); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="3" y1="6" x2="21" y2="6"/><line x1="3" y1="12" x2="15" y2="12"/><line x1="3" y1="18" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg> Summary</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleCite('cite2'); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M14.017 21v-7.391c0-5.704 3.731-9.57 8.983-10.609l.995 2.151c-2.432.917-3.995 3.638-3.995 5.849h4v10h-9.983zm-14.017 0v-7.391c0-5.704 3.748-9.57 9-10.609l.996 2.151c-2.433.917-3.996 3.638-3.996 5.849h3.983v10h-9.983z"/></svg> Cite</a>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-abstract" id="abs2">
      I develop an economic theory of candidate selection based on the supply of and demand for female candidates across parties on the left and the right. Using data for 11,431 women candidates from 61 elections in 23 Western parliamentary democracies between 2005 and 2021, I test theoretical expectations on five candidate-level characteristics from the Comparative Candidates Survey.
    </div>
    <div class="cite-block" id="cite2">
<pre>@article{abus2025gendered,
  title   = {The Gendered Politics of Candidate Selection:
             An Analysis of Left-Right Preferences in
             Parliamentary Democracies},
  author  = {Abus, Murat},
  journal = {Political Research Quarterly},
  year    = {2025},
  number  = {3},
  volume  = {78},
  pages   = {1060--1073}
}</pre>
      <button onclick="copyBib('cite2')">Copy</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-card">  <div class="pub-number">3.</div>
  <div class="pub-content">
    <div class="pub-title-line">
      <span class="pub-title">Local News, Partisanship, and Perceptions About Election Administration</span>
      <span class="pub-journal-inline">Electoral Studies (2025)</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-authors"><strong>Murat Abus</strong>, Kexin Bai &amp; Johanna Dunaway</div>
    <div class="pub-links">
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.electstud.2025.102970" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"/><polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"/><line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"/></svg> DOI</a>
      <a href="uploads/Abus et al_2025_Local News, Partisanship, and Perceptions about Election Administration.pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/><polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/><line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13"/><line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17"/></svg> PDF</a>
      <a href="uploads/local_news_partisanship_election_administration(S.I).pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M21.44 11.05l-9.19 9.19a6 6 0 0 1-8.49-8.49l9.19-9.19a4 4 0 0 1 5.66 5.66l-9.2 9.19a2 2 0 0 1-2.83-2.83l8.49-8.48"/></svg> Supplementary</a>
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/WEVPNI" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><ellipse cx="12" cy="5" rx="9" ry="3"/><path d="M21 12c0 1.66-4 3-9 3s-9-1.34-9-3"/><path d="M3 5v14c0 1.66 4 3 9 3s9-1.34 9-3V5"/></svg> Data &amp; Code</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleAbstract('abs3', this); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="3" y1="6" x2="21" y2="6"/><line x1="3" y1="12" x2="15" y2="12"/><line x1="3" y1="18" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg> Summary</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleCite('cite3'); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M14.017 21v-7.391c0-5.704 3.731-9.57 8.983-10.609l.995 2.151c-2.432.917-3.995 3.638-3.995 5.849h4v10h-9.983zm-14.017 0v-7.391c0-5.704 3.748-9.57 9-10.609l.996 2.151c-2.433.917-3.996 3.638-3.996 5.849h3.983v10h-9.983z"/></svg> Cite</a>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-abstract" id="abs3">
      We use individual-level survey data, measures of local news availability, and county-level election results from the 2016 and 2020 cycles to examine whether and how access to local news moderates the effects of partisanship and the winner/loser gap on voter perceptions of election integrity.
    </div>
    <div class="cite-block" id="cite3">
<pre>@article{abus2025localnews,
  title   = {Local News, Partisanship, and Perceptions About
             Election Administration},
  author  = {Abus, Murat and Bai, Kexin and Dunaway, Johanna},
  journal = {Electoral Studies},
  year    = {2025},
  pages   = {102970},
  volume  = {97}
}</pre>
      <button onclick="copyBib('cite3')">Copy</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-card">
  <div class="pub-number">2.</div>
  <div class="pub-content">
    <div class="pub-title-line">
      <span class="pub-title">A Theory of Gender's Effect on Vote Shift with a Test Based on Turkish Elections</span>
      <span class="pub-journal-inline">Mediterranean Politics (2024)</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-authors"><strong>Murat Abus</strong></div>
    <div class="pub-links">
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/13629395.2023.2194154" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"/><polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"/><line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"/></svg> DOI</a>
      <a href="uploads/a_theory_of_gender's_effect_on_vote_shift(S.I.).pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M21.44 11.05l-9.19 9.19a6 6 0 0 1-8.49-8.49l9.19-9.19a4 4 0 0 1 5.66 5.66l-9.2 9.19a2 2 0 0 1-2.83-2.83l8.49-8.48"/></svg> Supplementary</a>
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/FSAFCK" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><ellipse cx="12" cy="5" rx="9" ry="3"/><path d="M21 12c0 1.66-4 3-9 3s-9-1.34-9-3"/><path d="M3 5v14c0 1.66 4 3 9 3s9-1.34 9-3V5"/></svg> Data &amp; Code</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleAbstract('abs4', this); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="3" y1="6" x2="21" y2="6"/><line x1="3" y1="12" x2="15" y2="12"/><line x1="3" y1="18" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg> Summary</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleCite('cite4'); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M14.017 21v-7.391c0-5.704 3.731-9.57 8.983-10.609l.995 2.151c-2.432.917-3.995 3.638-3.995 5.849h4v10h-9.983zm-14.017 0v-7.391c0-5.704 3.748-9.57 9-10.609l.996 2.151c-2.433.917-3.996 3.638-3.996 5.849h3.983v10h-9.983z"/></svg> Cite</a>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-abstract" id="abs4">
      I theorize that when voters are faced with female candidates or female party leaders, their assessment of gender roles will be a deciding factor for whether an intended vote shift will be realized. Taking into account the spatial dependence of factors that affect voting behaviour, I empirically demonstrate that for two ideologically opposing parties, the salience of gender politics affected their vote share in the 2018 general election.
    </div>
    <div class="cite-block" id="cite4">
<pre>@article{abus2024gender,
  title   = {A Theory of Gender's Effect on Vote Shift with a
             Test Based on {Turkish} Elections},
  author  = {Abus, Murat},
  journal = {Mediterranean Politics},
  year    = {2024},
  volume  = {29},
  number  = {5},
  pages   = {729--754}
}</pre>
      <button onclick="copyBib('cite4')">Copy</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="pub-card">
  <div class="pub-number">1.</div>
  <div class="pub-content">
    <div class="pub-title-line">
      <span class="pub-title">Democratization in the Balkans, 1990–2002</span>
      <span class="pub-journal-inline">Alternatives: Turkish Journal of International Relations (2003)</span>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-authors"><strong>Murat Abus</strong></div>
    <div class="pub-links">
      <a href="uploads/Abus_2003 Democratization in the Balkans.pdf" target="_blank"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/><polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/><line x1="16" y1="13" x2="8" y2="13"/><line x1="16" y1="17" x2="8" y2="17"/></svg> PDF</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleAbstract('abs5', this); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="3" y1="6" x2="21" y2="6"/><line x1="3" y1="12" x2="15" y2="12"/><line x1="3" y1="18" x2="18" y2="18"/></svg> Summary</a>
      <a href="#" onclick="toggleCite('cite5'); return false;"><svg width="12" height="12" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M14.017 21v-7.391c0-5.704 3.731-9.57 8.983-10.609l.995 2.151c-2.432.917-3.995 3.638-3.995 5.849h4v10h-9.983zm-14.017 0v-7.391c0-5.704 3.748-9.57 9-10.609l.996 2.151c-2.433.917-3.996 3.638-3.996 5.849h3.983v10h-9.983z"/></svg> Cite</a>
    </div>
    <div class="pub-abstract" id="abs5">
      I argue that "gradualism" is a better way to approach the issue of democratization in the Balkans compared to frameworks that call for a clear separation between transition and consolidation. The results after a decade of democratization are decidedly mixed, but this region surpasses almost all other postcommunist regions with respect to the measures of institutionalization explored.
    </div>
    <div class="cite-block" id="cite5">
<pre>@article{abus2003democratization,
  title   = {Democratization in the {Balkans}, 1990--2002},
  author  = {Abus, Murat},
  journal = {Alternatives: {Turkish} Journal of
             International Relations},
  year    = {2003},
  volume  = {2},
  number  = {3 \& 4},
  pages   = {86--105}
}</pre>
      <button onclick="copyBib('cite5')">Copy</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="section-heading">Works in Progress</div>
<div class="wip-list">
  <div class="wip-item">"Electoral Consequences of Administrative Reforms"</div>
  <div class="wip-item">"Nationalization of Local Elections" <em>(with Cameron Wimpy)</em></div>
  <div class="wip-item">"Turnout in Local Elections" <em>(with Cameron Wimpy)</em></div>
  <div class="wip-item">"Local News and Political Competition" <em>(with Johanna Dunaway)</em></div>
  <div class="wip-item">"Municipalities and Territorial Reforms in the Context of Authoritarianism" <em>(with Sabri Ciftci)</em></div>
  <div class="wip-item">"Local Media Access and Voter Confidence" <em>(with Kexin Bai)</em></div>
</div>

</div>

<script>
function toggleCite(id) {
  var el = document.getElementById(id);
  el.style.display = el.style.display === 'block' ? 'none' : 'block';
}
function toggleAbstract(id, btn) {
  var el = document.getElementById(id);
  var isOpen = el.classList.contains('open');
  if (isOpen) {
    el.classList.remove('open');
    btn.classList.remove('active');
  } else {
    el.classList.add('open');
    btn.classList.add('active');
  }
}
function copyBib(id) {
  var text = document.getElementById(id).querySelector('pre').innerText;
  navigator.clipboard.writeText(text).then(function() {
    var btn = document.getElementById(id).querySelector('button');
    btn.textContent = 'Copied!';
    setTimeout(function() { btn.textContent = 'Copy'; }, 2000);
  });
}
</script>
```
